input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
With the server building and static files in place, wire up a real notes API backed by SQLite. The full contract lives in /app/docs/api_contract.md; follow that document for status codes, headers, JSON shapes, pagination, body size limits, and error bodies.

Persist data in /app/data/notes.db. Load the API secret from /app/config/api.key, trim whitespace, and require it in X-API-Key on POST, PUT, and DELETE. Reads must work without the key; wrong or missing keys on writes are unauthorized. Apply the same title validation rules on PUT as on POST, including rejecting empty titles on updates. Collection GET must support limit and offset paging and expose the pre-page total in the X-Total-Count header as spelled out in the contract file.

Keep JSON content type and CORS headers on API responses, honor OPTIONS preflight as described in the contract, and make sure concurrent writes do not drop rows when verifiers hammer the service in parallel. Leave static serving intact so /app.js and the rest of /app/www still come through notes_server on port 8080 when you are done, and tell me when the API work is complete before you start the browser script.


Current terminal state:
New Terminal Output:

200root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

New Terminal Output:

200root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# find /app -type f | head -50
/app/Makefile
/app/README.md
/app/config/api.key
/app/config/defaults.env
/app/data/notes.db
/app/deps/versions.txt
/app/docs/api_contract.md
/app/docs/build_notes.md
/app/docs/js_verifier_mock.md
/app/docs/runtime_layout.md
/app/include/common.h
/app/include/notes.h
/app/sql/init.sql
/app/src/auth.c
/app/src/auth.h
/app/src/db.c
/app/src/db.h
/app/src/main.c
/app/src/routes.c
/app/src/routes.h
/app/src/static.c
/app/src/static.h
/app/www/app.js
/app/www/index.html
/app/www/style.css
/app/notes_server
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# cat /app/docs/api_contract.md
This document is the normative REST contract for the notes API. Automated checks use arbitrary title and content strings in requests; only the behaviors, status
 codes, headers, and JSON field names here matter.

Store notes in /app/data/notes.db. Read the shared secret from /app/config/api.key and trim surrounding whitespace before comparing it to the X-API-Key header o
n mutating requests. The stock api.key file ships with tb3_notes_api_secret_2026. POST, PUT, and DELETE require a matching key or the server responds unauthoriz
ed. GET never requires the key; a bad or missing key on a mutating call is unauthorized.

Every JSON response must use a JSON content type and include an access-control header that allows any origin. OPTIONS preflight must advertise GET, POST, PUT, D
ELETE, and OPTIONS, and must allow Content-Type and X-API-Key as request headers. Preflight may return empty or non-empty success.

POST /api/notes accepts JSON with string title and content. Title must be present and non-empty; an empty object or missing or blank title is a client error. Su
ccess is HTTP 201 with a body containing numeric id plus stored title and content. GET /api/notes returns a JSON array of objects with id, title, and content. O
ptional limit and offset query parameters are integers: default limit 50, maximum 100, default offset 0. /api/notes?limit=100 returns at most one hundred rows;
/api/notes?limit=999 is capped the same way. The total row count before paging goes in the X-Total-Count header.

GET /api/notes/{id} returns that object. A missing id returns HTTP 404 with JSON {"error":"not found"} using that exact lowercase message string, including for
ids such as /api/notes/99999 on GET, PUT, or DELETE. PUT uses the same field rules as POST, returns HTTP 200 with saved fields when the row exists, or the same
not-found JSON when it does not. Successful DELETE returns HTTP 200 with JSON {"status":"deleted"}; deleting a missing id returns the same not-found JSON as GET
. Reject POST or PUT bodies larger than 65536 bytes with HTTP 413 and a JSON error body.

SQLite access must remain correct under concurrent requests. Verifiers may reset /app/data/notes.db and send bursts of about fifteen parallel POST requests; eve
ry stored row must still appear in responses afterward.
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

New Terminal Output:

root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# printf 'CC = gcc\nCFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -Iinclude\nLDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3 -lpthread\n\nSOURCES = src/main.c src/routes.c src/
db.c src/auth.c\nTARGET = notes_server\n\n$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)\n\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)\n\nclean:\n\trm -f $(TARGET)\n' > /app/Mak
efile
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# cat -A /app/Makefile
CC = gcc$
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -Iinclude$
LDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3 -lpthread$
$
SOURCES = src/main.c src/routes.c src/db.c src/auth.c$
TARGET = notes_server$
$
$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)$
^I$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)$
$
clean:$
^Irm -f $(TARGET)$
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# make clean && make 2>&1
rm -f notes_server
gcc -Wall -Wextra -Iinclude -o notes_server src/main.c src/routes.c src/db.c src/auth.c -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3 -lpthread
src/routes.c: In function ‘route_request’:
src/routes.c:169:13: warning: Value MHD_HTTP_PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE is deprecated use MHD_HTTP_CONTENT_TOO_LARGE
  169 |         return send_json(connection, "{\"error\":\"body too large\"}", MHD_HTTP_PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE);
      |             ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
src/routes.c: At top level:
src/routes.c:109:12: warning: ‘json_has_key’ defined but not used [-Wunused-function]
  109 | static int json_has_key(const char *json, const char *key) {
      |            ^~~~~~~~~~~~
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

ecret_2026' -d '{"title":"Test Note","content":"Hello world"}'
{"error":"not found"}

t":"test"}'
{"error":"not found"}
404root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s -w '\n%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/notes -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: tb3_notes_api_
secret_2026' -d '{"content":"no title"}'
{"error":"not found"}
404root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s -w '\n%{http_code}' -X PUT http://localhost:8080/api/notes/1 -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: tb3_notes_api
_secret_2026' -d '{"title":"Updated","content":"new content"}'
{"error":"not found"}
404root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s -w '\n%{http_code}' -X DELETE http://localhost:8080/api/notes/1 -H 'X-API-Key: tb3_notes_api_secret_2026'
{"error":"not found"}
404root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s -w '\n%{http_code}' http://localhost:8080/api/notes/1
{"error":"not found"}

  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
With the server building and static files in place, wire up a real notes API backed by SQLite. The full contract lives in /app/docs/api_contract.md; follow that document for status codes, headers, JSON shapes, pagination, body size limits, and error bodies.

Persist data in /app/data/notes.db. Load the API secret from /app/config/api.key, trim whitespace, and require it in X-API-Key on POST, PUT, and DELETE. Reads must work without the key; wrong or missing keys on writes are unauthorized. Apply the same title validation rules on PUT as on POST, including rejecting empty titles on updates. Collection GET must support limit and offset paging and expose the pre-page total in the X-Total-Count header as spelled out in the contract file.

Keep JSON content type and CORS headers on API responses, honor OPTIONS preflight as described in the contract, and make sure concurrent writes do not drop rows when verifiers hammer the service in parallel. Leave static serving intact so /app.js and the rest of /app/www still come through notes_server on port 8080 when you are done, and tell me when the API work is complete before you start the browser script.

root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# cat /app/docs/api_contract.md
This document is the normative REST contract for the notes API. Automated checks use arbitrary title and content strings in requests; only the behaviors, status
 codes, headers, and JSON field names here matter.

Store notes in /app/data/notes.db. Read the shared secret from /app/config/api.key and trim surrounding whitespace before comparing it to the X-API-Key header o
n mutating requests. The stock api.key file ships with tb3_notes_api_secret_2026. POST, PUT, and DELETE require a matching key or the server responds unauthoriz
ed. GET never requires the key; a bad or missing key on a mutating call is unauthorized.

Every JSON response must use a JSON content type and include an access-control header that allows any origin. OPTIONS preflight must advertise GET, POST, PUT, D
ELETE, and OPTIONS, and must allow Content-Type and X-API-Key as request headers. Preflight may return empty or non-empty success.

POST /api/notes accepts JSON with string title and content. Title must be present and non-empty; an empty object or missing or blank title is a client error. Su
ccess is HTTP 201 with a body containing numeric id plus stored title and content. GET /api/notes returns a JSON array of objects with id, title, and content. O
ptional limit and offset query parameters are integers: default limit 50, maximum 100, default offset 0. /api/notes?limit=100 returns at most one hundred rows;
/api/notes?limit=999 is capped the same way. The total row count before paging goes in the X-Total-Count header.

GET /api/notes/{id} returns that object. A missing id returns HTTP 404 with JSON {"error":"not found"} using that exact lowercase message string, including for
ids such as /api/notes/99999 on GET, PUT, or DELETE. PUT uses the same field rules as POST, returns HTTP 200 with saved fields when the row exists, or the same
not-found JSON when it does not. Successful DELETE returns HTTP 200 with JSON {"status":"deleted"}; deleting a missing id returns the same not-found JSON as GET
. Reject POST or PUT bodies larger than 65536 bytes with HTTP 413 and a JSON error body.

SQLite access must remain correct under concurrent requests. Verifiers may reset /app/data/notes.db and send bursts of about fifteen parallel POST requests; eve
ry stored row must still appear in responses afterward.
